rationale: The citing paper gives the Che, Sudhir, and Seetharaman (2007) study a specific role in framing its contribution: it identifies that paper as examining firms' look-ahead behavior under state-dependent demand. It then explicitly contrasts its own objective as analyzing how consumer loyalty/switching costs affect equilibrium prices, a question it says Che et al. do not address. This is more than a passing name-check because the citation is used to define the research gap and motivate the paper's core contribution, even though methods are not directly reused.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the prior line on state-dependent-demand pricing by adding an analysis of how consumer loyalty/switching costs shape competitive equilibrium prices. It positions Che et al. (2007) as related prior work on look-ahead behavior and then advances a different but directly connected question.
